topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

The meticulously bound first edition lay on my desk, a monument to years of devotion. I traced the gilded lettering with a fingertip, a sudden, cold knot tightening in my chest. It wasn't just a resemblance; it was a chilling, deliberate mirroring of my most intimate narratives. I reread the passage, the familiar rhythm of my own prose now twisted into something hollow and borrowed. A tremor ran through my hand, making the book tremble slightly. I pushed it away with a sharp, decisive movement, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet room. I needed air. I needed to dismantle this deception piece by piece. Every word felt like a calculated slight, a violation of the very soul I poured onto those pages.

[story 2]

"You couldn't just take that," I hissed, the words scraping raw from my throat. My jaw was clenched so tightly I could feel the strain in my molars. Across the table, his practiced, smooth demeanor didn't falter, offering a weak, patronizing smile. But I saw the flicker in his eyes—a momentary acknowledgment of the sheer audacity of his maneuver. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of the mahogany. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping backward on the floor, the noise a deliberate punctuation mark to my rising agitation. I didn't shout; I just stared, my gaze unwavering, letting the sheer force of my disapproval settle between us like a physical weight.

[story 3]

The ink seemed to swim before my eyes, the familiar constellations of my imagination suddenly tainted by a foreign, clumsy hand. I paced the length of the studio, my boots clicking a frantic, uneven rhythm against the hardwood. My breath hitched in shallow, rapid bursts. It wasn't just a professional slight; it felt like a personal trespass, a theft of my inner landscape. I stopped dead in front of the manuscript, my chest heaving. I snatched up a red pen, the plastic feeling slick and hot in my palm. With a swift, almost violent motion, I slashed a thick, black line through a carefully crafted sentence, the paper protesting beneath the pressure. The sound of the scratch was sharp, a declaration of absolute refusal.
